data.title: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Graphjet Technology, a company specializing in the production of artificial graphene and graphite using a patented process that converts palm kernel shells into high-quality materials, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive graphite and graphene industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $0.0928 million, an increase resulting from selling side products since June 2025.
  • Gross Loss: $0.1002 million, primarily due to low productivity levels and discounted sales of deteriorated side products.
  • Loss from Operations: $22.522 million, a 29.2% increase from the previous year, mainly due to share compensation expenses.
  • Net Loss: $16.411 million, reduced by approximately $1.4 million or 8% from the previous year, mainly due to the reversal of bonus provision.
  • Loss per Share: $(6.49), reflecting the company's financial performance and the impact of share compensation expenses.

Business Highlights

  • Graphjet Technology Overview: Specializes in producing artificial graphene and graphite from palm kernel shells, offering a cost-effective production method compared to traditional processes.
  • Market Opportunity: The global graphite market is expected to grow significantly, driven by demand from the lithium-ion battery industry. Graphjet aims to fill the supply gap for graphite and graphene, critical for EV batteries.
  • Supply of Raw Materials: Relies on abundant palm kernel shells in Malaysia, with plans to construct its first main production facility in the country.
  • New Production Launches: Working towards completing a production facility in Malaysia and planning a commercial artificial graphite production facility in Nevada, expected to produce up to 10,000 metric tons of battery-grade graphite annually.
  • Strategic Partnerships: Entered into a supply agreement with Toyoda to provide $30 million worth of graphite and graphene annually for their carbon-neutral mobility products.
  • Research and Development: Collaborates with prestigious institutions like the National University of Malaysia and University Teknikal Malaysia Melaka to advance its technology and applications in graphite and graphene.
  • Future Outlook: Plans to expand manufacturing capabilities in Malaysia and North America, aiming to become a leading supplier of high-quality, cost-effective artificial graphite and graphene. Exploring strategic partnerships and government incentives to support growth and market entry.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Transition to Commercialization: Focused on completing system integration, process stability validation, and establishing production protocols. Strengthening engineering, production, and quality assurance teams to support long-term operational reliability and regulatory compliance.
  • Capital Management: Engaged in several capital management activities, including issuing ordinary shares and warrants to raise funds and settle debts. Completed a reverse recapitalization and received a $2.5 million PIPE investment. Executed debt settlement agreements and issued shares to settle amounts due to shareholders and creditors. Pursuing a diversified funding approach, including strategic partnerships, asset-based financing, and potential access to capital market instruments. Class A ordinary shares were delisted from Nasdaq and are now quoted on the OTC Pink Limited marketplace, impacting liquidity and marketability.
  • Future Outlook: Plans to leverage government incentive programs to support sustainable manufacturing and technology commercialization. Aiming to execute a structured capacity-expansion program following successful commissioning and initial product delivery. Committed to securing necessary capital to finalize commissioning activities and support growth plans, focusing on maintaining financial flexibility and aligning financing resources with strategic priorities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Limited Operating History: The company has a very limited operating history, making it difficult to evaluate its success and future viability.
  • Going Concern Doubts: The independent registered public accounting firm’s report contains an explanatory paragraph expressing substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern due to net losses and liabilities exceeding assets.
  • Graphene/Graphite Manufacturing Risks: The proposed manufacturing business carries risks such as unanticipated liabilities, the need for additional capital, and competition from better-funded companies.
  • Competitive Industry: The graphene and graphite industry is highly competitive, with competition based on price, performance, and innovation.
  • Intellectual Property Risks: The company may face challenges in protecting its intellectual property, which could undermine its competitive position.
  • Dependence on Palm Oil Industry: The availability of raw materials like palm kernel shells is contingent on the palm oil industry, which is subject to price volatility and other external factors.
  • International Operations Risks: The company’s international operations expose it to risks such as political instability, regulatory changes, and currency fluctuations.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations is complex and costly. Non-compliance could result in significant liabilities and affect operations.
  • Cybersecurity Threats: The company is vulnerable to cyber-attacks and IT system failures, which could result in data breaches and operational disruptions.
  • Nasdaq Listing Compliance: The company has received multiple notices from Nasdaq regarding non-compliance with listing rules, including minimum bid price and market value requirements. Failure to regain compliance could result in delisting, affecting liquidity and stock price.

SEC Filing: GRAPHJET TECHNOLOGY [ GTIJF ] - 10-K - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: Jupiter Neurosciences Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
Jupiter Neurosciences executed financing arrangements with Yorkville to strengthen liquidity. The company signed a Standby Equity Purchase Agreement on Oct 24, 2025, allowing it to sell shares to Yorkville over time within trading-volume limits. A related Registration Rights Agreement supported the filing of a resale registration statement, which was declared effective on Dec 11, 2025. Shareholders approved issuances above Nasdaq’s 19.99% cap on Dec 19, 2025, and the company received a $1.86 million second pre-paid advance on Dec 23, 2025. These steps are intended to provide flexible access to capital for operations and growth.

Agreement 1: Jupiter Neurosciences Signs Standby Equity Purchase Agreement With Yorkville

  • Agreement type: Standby Equity Purchase Agreement
  • Counterparty: Yorkville
  • Signed / Effective: Oct 24 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enhance liquidity and financing flexibility

Agreement 2: Jupiter Neurosciences Signs Registration Rights Agreement With Yorkville

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement
  • Counterparty: Yorkville
  • Signed / Effective: Oct 24 2025 / Dec 11 2025
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enable resale registration for SEPA shares

Original SEC Filing: JUPITER NEUROSCIENCES, INC. [ JUNS ] - 8-K - Dec. 23, 2025

On December 23, 2025, Bausch Health Companies Inc announced the final results of its exchange offers for senior secured notes.

data.title: Some Items Are Getting a Second Life as Christmas Presents — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Some 82% of roughly 1,800 U.S. shoppers surveyed by eBay said they are more likely to purchase secondhand gifts this year compared with last year. Likewise, nearly half of the 8,200 American consumers the National Retail Federation surveyed said they might buy used to save money this holiday season.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Amazon Blocks Job Applications Linked To North Korean Operatives
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - A senior Amazon executive has revealed that the company has blocked more than 1,800 job applications believed to be linked to North Korean agents attempting to infiltrate the US tech workforce.

According to Amazon's chief security officer, Stephen Schmidt, the individuals were applying for remote IT roles using stolen or fabricated identities.

In a LinkedIn post, Schmidt said the suspected operatives aimed to secure employment, earn salaries, and funnel the income back to North Korea to help fund the regime's weapons programs.

He warned that the issue is likely widespread across the technology sector, particularly in the United States, where remote work has expanded hiring opportunities.

Amazon has recorded nearly a one-third increase in suspected North Korean job applications over the past year. Schmidt noted that these workers often rely on so-called 'laptop farms,' where computers physically located in the US are remotely operated from abroad to disguise their true location.

The company uses a mix of artificial intelligence tools and human review to screen applicants, but Schmidt cautioned that fraud tactics are becoming increasingly sophisticated. These include hijacking dormant LinkedIn accounts and impersonating real software engineers to appear legitimate.

US authorities have also highlighted the threat. The Department of Justice recently uncovered dozens of illegal laptop farms and prosecuted individuals who helped North Korean IT workers secure jobs, generating millions of dollars in illicit revenue for Pyongyang.

Copyright(c) 2025 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Mercedes-Benz Agrees To Pay To Settle Diesel Emissions Cheating Allegations
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Mercedes-Benz USA and parent company Daimer AG have agreed to pay $149.6 million to settle allegations that the automaker secretly installed devices in hundreds of thousands of vehicles to pass emission tests, a coalition of attorneys general announced Monday.

According to the coalition, between 2008 and 2016, the German automaker equipped more than 211,000 diesel passenger cars and vans with software devices that optimized emission controls during tests but reduced the controls during normal operations.

The devices enabled vehicles to far exceed legal limits for nitrogen oxides, a pollutant that can cause respiratory illnesses and contributes to smog.

The states alleged that Mercedes installed the devices because it couldn't reach design and performance goals such as fuel efficiency while complying with emissions standards. The automaker allegedly concealed the devices from state and federal regulators and the public while marketing the vehicles as 'environmentally friendly' and compliant with emissions standards.

The agreement is still subject to court approval.

Daimler AG and Mercedes-Benz USA already agreed in 2020 to pay $1.5 billion to the U.S. government and California state regulators to resolve the emissions cheating allegations.

Mercedes-Benz issued a statement saying the deal announced Monday will resolve all remaining legal proceedings tied to diesel emissions in the United States, but the company still considers the accusations unfounded and denies any liability. The automaker has made 'sufficient provisions' for the cost of the settlement, the statement said.

Statement calls for the automaker to pay the attorneys general $120 million with another $29 million payment suspended and potentially waived pending completion of a consumer relief program.

That effort will extend to the roughly 40,000 vehicles with the devices that hadn't been repaired or permanently removed from the road by Aug. 1, 2023.

The owners of those vehicles would get $2,000 per vehicle if they install approved emissions modification software and an extended warranty.

The settlement also calls for Mercedes to comply with reporting requirements and refrain from any further unfair or deceptive marketing or sale of diesel vehicles.

Volkswagen also ended up paying $2.8 billion to settle a criminal case due to emissions cheating.

Copyright(c) 2025 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Bausch Health Announces Final Results and Expiration of Exchange Offers
data.description:
LAVAL, QC / ACCESS Newswire / December 23, 2025 / Bausch Health Companies Inc. (the "Company") and its subsidiary 1261229 B.C. Ltd. (the "Issuer" and, together with the Company, the "Offerors") today announced the final results and expiration of their previously announced offers to exchange the Company's outstanding 4.875% Senior Secured Notes due 2028 (the "4.875% Notes") and 11.00% Senior Secured Notes due 2028 (the "11.00% Notes" and together with the 4.875% Notes, the "Existing Senior Secured Notes") for up to $1.6 billion aggregate principal amount of the Issuer's 10.00% Senior Secured Notes due 2032 (the "New Notes" and, such offers, the "Offers") , in each case, pursuant to the terms described in a confidential exchange offer memorandum dated November 24, 2025 (the "Exchange Offer Memorandum").

The Exchange Offers expired at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on December 23, 2025 (the "Expiration Time"). As reported by D.F. King & Co., Inc., the exchange agent and information agent for the Offers, as of the Expiration Time, an aggregate principal amount of $2.7 billion of Existing Senior Secured Notes had been validly tendered (and not validly withdrawn) in the Offers, as set forth in the table below.

Series of Existing

(1) No representation is made as to the correctness or accuracy of the CUSIP numbers listed in this press release or printed on the Existing Senior Secured Notes. They are provided solely for convenience.

(2) Consideration in the form of principal amount of the New Notes, per $1,000 principal amount of Existing Senior Secured Notes that are validly tendered and accepted for exchange.

(3) The Total Consideration of $1,020.00 per $1,000 principal amount of 11.00% Notes excludes accrued interest paid in cash as provided in the Exchange Offer Memorandum. The Total Consideration of $873.84 per $1,000 principal amount of 4.875% Notes has been adjusted to reflect the net interest payment resulting from the difference between (i) the accrued interest due to each holder of 4.875% Notes who has tendered 4.875% Notes into the Offer and whose tenders have been accepted in the Offers and (ii) the accrued and unpaid interest due on the New Notes to be issued in respect thereof from the last interest payment date of the existing 10.00% Senior Secured Notes due 2032 to, but not including, the settlement date of the Offers.

Subject to the terms and conditions of the Offers set forth in the Exchange Offer Memorandum, upon settlement of the Offers, which is currently expected to occur on December 26, 2025 (the "Settlement Date"), there will be approximately $1,600 million principal amount of New Notes issued in respect of the Existing Senior Secured Notes to be accepted in the Offers, as set forth in the table above. All eligible holders who have certified to the Offerors that they are eligible to participate in the Offers and whose Existing Senior Secured Notes are accepted for exchange pursuant to the Offers on the Settlement Date will also be paid a cash amount, if applicable, equal to the difference between (i) the accrued interest due to each holder of Existing Senior Secured Notes accepted in the Offers and (ii) the accrued and unpaid interest due on the New Notes from the last interest payment date of the existing 10.00% Senior Secured Notes due 2032 to, but not including, the Settlement Date.

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP and Norton Rose Fulbright Canada LLP acted as legal counsel, and Evercore Inc. acted as financial advisor to the Company.

This press release does not constitute an offer to buy or sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy or sell any securities, nor shall there be any sale of securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such jurisdiction. The New Notes have not been and will not be registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "Securities Act") or any state securities laws, and may not be offered or sold in the United States absent registration or an applicable exemption from registration under the Securities Act and applicable state securities laws. The New Notes have not been and will not be qualified for sale to the public by prospectus under applicable Canadian securities laws and, accordingly, any offer and sale of the New Notes in Canada will be made on a basis, which is exempt from the prospectus requirements of such securities laws.

About Bausch Health

Bausch Health Companies Inc. is a global, diversified pharmaceutical company enriching lives through our relentless drive to deliver better health care outcomes. We develop, manufacture and market a range of products primarily in gastroenterology, hepatology, neurology, dermatology, dentistry, aesthetics, international pharmaceuticals and eye health, through our controlling interest in Bausch + Lomb Corporation. Our ambition is to be a globally integrated healthcare company, trusted and valued by patients, HCPs, employees and investors. Visit www.bauschhealth.com for more information.

Forward‐Looking Statements About Bausch Health

This news release may contain forward-looking statements within the meaning of applicable securities laws, including the Safe Harbor provisions of the U.S. Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. data.title: ADM Endeavors Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
ADM Endeavors signed two related agreements with GHS Investments to support equity financing. The Equity Financing Agreement permits GHS to purchase up to $20 million of common stock in tranches up to $500,000, priced at a discount to market and subject to registration effectiveness and ownership limits. ADM Endeavors also issued 1,156,738 shares as a commitment fee. Complementing this, the Registration Rights Agreement requires the company to register shares issuable under the facility, initially up to 29,000,000 shares, with filing targeted within 30 days and SEC effectiveness within 90 days after filing. Together, these agreements aim to provide flexible access to capital and facilitate share resales.

Agreement 1: ADM Endeavors Signs Equity Financing Agreement With GHS Investments

  • Agreement type: Equity financing facility for periodic common stock purchases
  • Counterparty: GHS Investments
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 19 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide flexible access to growth capital

Agreement 2: ADM Endeavors Signs Registration Rights Agreement With GHS Investments

  • Agreement type: Registration rights agreement for equity financing shares
  • Counterparty: GHS Investments
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 19 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enable resale and facilitate financing execution

Original SEC Filing: ADM ENDEAVORS, INC. [ ADMQ ] - 8-K - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: GM Investigating Air Bag That Exploded in Recalled-But-Unrepaired Pickup Truck — WSJ
data.description:
By Ryan Felton

General Motors and federal auto regulators are probing the crash of a pickup that injured a passenger after its air bag ruptured.

The automaker and regulators are examining what happened when a passenger in a GM pickup was injured by the improper deployment of the truck's air bags during a collision, people familiar with the matter said.

The GMC Sierra pickup, from the 2010 model year, originally used air bags made by the firm Takata that have been recalled, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said.

The date and location of the incident couldn't be immediately learned. Nor have investigators confirmed whether the air bag was made by Takata, rather than one from another manufacturer that may have been installed later, one of the people said.

GM recalled GMC Sierra pickups for the air bag issue in 2021.

"We have not gained access to inspect the vehicle, which is critical to understanding what occurred, and it would be premature to draw conclusions at this stage," a GM spokesman said. He said customer safety is the company's highest priority.

NHTSA said it was gathering information about the rupture of an air bag in a 2010 GMC Sierra with an open Takata recall.

"Recalled, unrepaired Takata air bags are dangerous and pose an immediate risk, as occupants have been seriously injured or killed in otherwise survivable crashes," the agency said.

At least 12 people have died in crashes involving vehicles that were recalled between 2015 and 2024 due to defective air bags but hadn't yet been repaired, The Wall Street Journal reported this month. Millions of cars recalled between 2015 and 2024 for air bags that may not deploy remain unfixed, the Journal reported.

Once a manufacturer of seat belts, Japan-based Takata became a major supplier of air bags. But its products used a chemical in the propellants inflating the air bags that became unstable and led to ruptures after aging and prolonged exposure to heat and humidity.

Recalls of Takata air bags are one of the largest of its kind in automotive history. They have been ongoing for more than a decade and cover more than 70 million air bags in roughly 42 million cars made by a range of car companies.

They also contributed to the bankruptcy of Takata, which is now defunct.

NHTSA to date has confirmed 28 deaths in the U.S. after defective Takata air bags exploded.

To date, GM has repaired about 50% of the air bags covered by its Takata recalls, according to the latest available NHTSA data.

Write to Ryan Felton at ryan.felton@wsj.com

data.title: Voip-pal.com Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
VoIP-Pal.Com Inc., a Nevada-based company specializing in Voice-over-Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies, has released its Form 10-K report for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive telecommunications industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 million. The company reported no revenues for the fiscal years ending September 30, 2025, and 2024.
  • Gross Margin: $0 million. The company had no gross margin for the fiscal years ending September 30, 2025, and 2024.
  • Net Loss: $6.23 million. The net loss decreased by $3.95 million, or 39%, compared to the previous year, primarily due to a reduction in legal fees and officer and director fees.
  • Basic and Diluted Loss Per Common Share: $0.00. The weighted-average number of common shares outstanding, basic and diluted, was 3,656,152,334 for the year ended September 30, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Company Overview: VoIP-Pal.Com Inc., incorporated in Nevada in 1997, focuses on developing VoIP technologies. The company has evolved through various business stages, including becoming a VoIP re-seller and developing a proprietary billing platform and anti-virus applications for smartphones.
  • Patent Portfolio: The company owns a comprehensive portfolio of patents covering technologies such as communication classification and routing, lawful intercept, emergency calling support, mobile gateways, uninterrupted transmission during endpoint changes, and metering and billing. These patents are crucial for its operations in the broadband VoIP market.
  • Litigation Activities: VoIP-Pal is actively involved in patent infringement lawsuits against several Fortune 500 companies, alleging unauthorized use of its patented technologies. The company is also engaged in antitrust lawsuits against major companies in the telecommunications industry.
  • Operational Focus: The company's operations are centered around monetizing, selling, and licensing its VoIP patents. This includes pursuing legal actions to protect its intellectual property rights and seeking licensing agreements with other companies.
  • Research and Development: For the fiscal years ending September 30, 2025, and 2024, VoIP-Pal did not incur any research and development expenses, indicating a focus on leveraging existing technologies and patents rather than developing new ones.
  • Employee Structure: VoIP-Pal does not have any employees and relies on consultants and contractors for its operations, reflecting a lean operational model focused on flexibility and cost management.
  • Emerging Growth Company Status: VoIP-Pal qualifies as an 'emerging growth company' under the JOBS Act, allowing it to benefit from reduced reporting requirements and exemptions from certain financial disclosure and governance mandates.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging its patent portfolio to generate revenue through licensing and litigation. However, the ongoing legal battles and the need for additional capital to fund operations pose significant challenges to its future growth and sustainability.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Monetizing VoIP Patents: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. has been focusing on monetizing its VoIP patents through licensing and sales, which is a core part of its strategic initiatives. The company is also engaged in ongoing litigation to protect its intellectual property, which is crucial for its business model.
  • Capital Management: The company primarily finances its operations through private placements of common stock and the exercise of warrants. During the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025, VoIP-Pal.com Inc. raised $550,000 through the issuance of 110,000,000 common shares. The company also issued 5,000,000 common shares for services and settled accounts payable with 5,000,000 common shares. As of September 30, 2025, the company had working capital of $969,267, a decrease from the previous year, indicating a need for additional capital to fund operations.
  • Future Outlook: VoIP-Pal.com Inc. anticipates the need for additional capital to fund its operations over the next 12 months. The company plans to continue its strategy of raising capital through private placements and the exercise of warrants. The ongoing litigation related to its intellectual property is expected to have a significant impact on its future operations and capital needs. The company is also exploring opportunities to monetize its patent suite, which could provide additional revenue streams and improve its financial position.

Challenges and Risks

  • Reliance on Litigation: VoIP-Pal.Com Inc. is primarily focused on monetizing its patent portfolio, which forms the core of its business strategy. The company is actively pursuing patent infringement lawsuits against several Fortune 500 companies, which presents both an opportunity and a risk. The outcome of these legal proceedings is uncertain and could significantly impact the company's financial position and operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company reported no revenues for the years ending September 30, 2025, and 2024, highlighting a critical operational risk related to its ability to generate income. The decrease in general and administrative expenses was primarily due to reduced legal fees and officer and director fees, indicating cost management efforts. However, the company continues to incur significant net losses, which may affect its liquidity and capital resources. The company relies on private placements and warrant exercises for financing, which may not be sustainable in the long term.
  • Market Risks: The company's financial condition is vulnerable to market fluctuations, particularly in the telecommunications and patent litigation sectors. The lack of revenue generation and dependence on legal outcomes for financial stability are significant market risks.

SEC Filing: Voip-pal.com Inc [ VPLM ] - 10-K - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: Amazon's Zoox Recalls 332 Vehicles Over ADS Software Error
data.description:
SEATTLE (dpa-AFX) - Zoox, the Amazon-owned (AMZN) autonomous vehicle company, has issued a voluntary software recall covering its entire active U.S. Fleet.

In a filing with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, Zoox said it has recalled 332 vehicles equipped with its automated driving system (ADS) software.

The recall applies to software released and installed between March 13 and December 18, 2025, and impacts both driver-operated test vehicles and fully driverless robotaxis operating on public roads.

According to the report, Zoox vehicles in certain situations crossed lane lines unnecessarily at or near intersections. Zoox said no crashes, injuries, or fatalities have been reported in connection with the issue.

The company traced the behavior to multiple software-related causes, including misclassification of double-parked vehicles, unexpected routing changes, and poorly executed maneuvers intended to avoid blocking cross traffic at intersections.

Zoox first identified the issue after an incident on August 26, 2025, involving a robotaxi that made a wide turn into an opposing lane. Over the following months, the company logged 62 similar instances and worked with regulators while refining a fix. Partial improvements were rolled out in November, followed by a more comprehensive software update finalized in mid-December.

As of December 19, 2025, Zoox stated that all affected vehicles operating on public roads have been updated, thereby completing the recall. Because Zoox owns and operates its fleet, no customer or dealer notifications were required.

data.title: Australia Shares Poised to Pause Recent Rally — Market Talk
data.description: Australia's S&P/ASX 200 seems poised to give back some of its recent strong gains at the start of its final pre-Christmas trading session. ASX futures are down by 0.1% ahead of Wednesday's session, which will end an hour earlier than usual. The benchmark index is up by 2.0% so far this week amid lower-volume trade, helped higher by strength in commodity stocks. Ahead of the open, infrastructure-services provider Ventia and defense-tech manufacturer DroneShield both announced new contracts. U.S. stocks provided a positive lead. The S&P 500 rose 0.5% to a record, the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.6%, and the DJIA edged 0.2% higher. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Basis bids flat to weaker ahead of holiday
data.description:
Spot basis bids for hard red winter (HRW) wheat stayed flat or lower at elevators in the southern U.S. Plains on Tuesday, according to dealers.

  • Dealers said trade was very slow with the Christmas holiday approaching.

  • Dry weather continued for Southern Plains wheat, according to Commodity Weather Group.

  • Protein premiums fell for wheat with 11.0% to 11.8% protein and for wheat with 13.8% and 14.0% protein on Tuesday, and were unchanged for all other grades.

  • K.C. March hard red winter wheat futures (KWH26) were up 6-3/4 cents at $5.28 a bushel.
